item.title: The Lost Horizon of John and Carolyn
item.description: “Love Story” is restoring Camelot’s sheen, tarnished by R.F.K. Jr.

item.title: I Love the Movies. Here’s How to Save Them.
item.description: The industry is still recovering from the upheavals of the pandemic era. But there’s one simple step to take right now that can ensure a bright future ahead.

item.title: Does Trump Risk Turning America Into a Rogue State?
item.description: The United States helped create safeguards to limit the brutality of war, and now I fear President Trump is dismantling them.

item.title: ‘This Spells Real Trouble for Republicans’
item.description: The Republican pollster Kristen Soltis Anderson joins E.J. Dionne Jr. and Robert Siegel to discuss Trump’s falling approval rating and what it portends for November.

item.title: What Trump Didn’t Know About Iran
item.description: Ali Vaez, the Iran project director at the International Crisis Group, explains the history of miscalculations that led to the war in Iran.

item.title: Social Media Isn’t Just Speech. It’s Also a Defective, Hazardous Product.
item.description: Don’t let the First Amendment arguments fool you.

item.title: The Political Cost of Trump’s War
item.description: The Republican pollster Kristen Soltis Anderson joins E.J. Dionne Jr. and Robert Siegel to discuss Trump’s falling approval rating and what it portends for November.

item.title: The Democrats Could Still Mess This Up
item.description: MS NOW anchor Chris Hayes says the party still can’t articulate its vision.

item.title: Trump’s Way of Doing Business With the World May Cost All of Us
item.description: Either the budget changes, or America’s relationship with the rest of the world does.

item.title: Europe Is in Great Danger. It Has Itself to Blame.
item.description: European politicians have colluded in their dependency on a rogue America.

item.title: I Went to Florida to See the 31-Year-Old Candidate Thrilling Gen Z. We’re in Trouble.
item.description: James Fishback has managed to bring the paranoid, transgressive, meme-drunk spirit of the right-wing internet into the real world.

item.title: Why I’m Suing Grammarly
item.description: A tech company made a deepfake of my mind. I’m fighting back.

item.title: China’s Plan to Spur Consumer Spending Is a Mirage
item.description: China may never be able to realize its longtime promise to shift away from an overreliance on exports.

item.title: Any Way You Look at It, Netanyahu Wins
item.description: The war in Iran is a victory for the Israeli prime minister.

item.title: Carolyn Bessette Was Living the Dream. Then She Met John.
item.description: The fairy tale was 1990s New York.

item.title: What Are We Living Through in Trump 2.0? Here Are 3 Possibilities.
item.description: On the modes of authoritarian crisis, more of the same and constitutional regime change.

item.title: The Rise of Anxiety Cinema
item.description: Some movies make us laugh or cry or feel scared. A new wave of jittery cinema seems designed to make us feel incredibly stressed out.

item.title: Mussolini Would Have Loved Trump’s Ballroom
item.description: Trump’s plans for Washington bring to mind what Mussolini did — and tried to do — to Rome.

item.title: Why the Kids Won’t Farm
item.description: Many young people want to farm. They just can’t afford it.

item.title: The Death Penalty Is Indefensible. It’s Also on the Rise.
item.description: The surge in capital punishment is a cruel and unjust development.

item.title: ‘One Battle After Another’ Is Just a Movie. Resistance Is Real.
item.description: Readers respond to a guest essay about the Paul Thomas Anderson movie “One Battle After Another” by the daughter of two Weatherman. Also: Will we be around to unearth a time capsule?

item.title: Trump and Netanyahu Are No Longer on the Same Page
item.description: American goals are starting to conflict with Israel’s.

item.title: The Trump Administration Floats a New Way to Humiliate the Legal Profession
item.description: The consequences would be grave.

item.title: Trump Is the Anti-Trump
item.description: There is an alternate universe in which the president is the popular, successful figure of his imagination.

item.title: Why So Much of New York Loves Mamdani
item.description: The new mayor is having a honeymoon with New Yorkers. Will it last?

item.title: A.I. Is Changing the Way We Think About Good and Evil
item.description: Chatbots can teach us about human nature.

item.title: Finding Fault for the Deadly U.S. Strike on a School in Iran
item.description: Readers react to the news that a U.S. missile likely killed 175 Iranians. Also: Kennedy pushes nutrition advice on medical schools; city life for retirees.

item.title: This Is the Democrats’ Biggest Problem
item.description: The fight over ICE actions is a symptom of a much larger, more fundamental debate happening in America, MS NOW’s Chris Hayes argues on “Interesting Times.”

item.title: ‘The Libbiest Lib Who Ever Libbed’
item.description: Gavin Newsom is more like Hillary Clinton than Barack Obama, and that’s bad news for any presidential aspirations Newsom may have, MS NOW’s Chris Hayes argues on “Interesting Times.”

item.title: I Declare War on You
item.description: Our constitutional framework for war lies in pieces around us.

item.title: ‘He Seems Really Bad at Getting Advice’: 3 Opinion Writers on Trump and Iran
item.description: Is Trump triumphing or has he committed a grave error? A debate on the Iran war.

item.title: It’s Oscars Snubbing Season Again
item.description: In a culture that can’t concentrate, perhaps our fixation on Oscar snubs is healthy.

item.title: Trump Isn’t Ready for What He’s Starting in Cuba
item.description: Catastrophe in Cuba is not a foregone conclusion.

item.title: If the Economy Is Improving, Why Is Your Budget So Tight? Tell Us Your Story.
item.description: Middle-class Americans are surviving, but not thriving. We want to know why.

item.title: The Democrats Could Still Mess This Up
item.description: Will they finally capitalize on Trump’s chaos?

item.title: Daryl Hannah’s Objections to ‘Love Story’
item.description: Readers respond to Daryl Hannah’s guest essay about a TV series’ portrayal of her. Also: Remembering a visit to Iran; an exercise plan for life.

item.title: Here’s One Boast Trump Won’t Be Making Anymore
item.description: The administration’s attacks on renewable energy mean higher costs for everyone.

item.title: A Kurdish Rebellion in Iran Could Be a Disaster
item.description: The United States and Israel have considered using Iranian Kurds as a ground force.

item.title: How Does This End? Four Scenarios for What Comes Next With Iran.
item.description: The best hope? Weaken the regime’s ability to lash out and resist an uprising.

item.title: The Smash-and-Grab Presidency Reaches Its Apex
item.description: When disruption becomes destruction.

item.title: Early Telephones Were Scary, but No One Panicked
item.description: The early telephone didn’t spark panic. That doesn’t mean we’re wrong to panic about smartphones.

item.title: It’s Trump’s War. We’re Stuck With the Bill.
item.description: What are we getting for all of President Trump’s military spending?

item.title: Teens Are Falling Out of Love With Tech
item.description: The next generation is more tech-skeptical than you think.

item.title: Why Voters Might Finally Blame Trump for Rising Costs
item.description: Trump’s Iran war will affect more than just gas prices.

item.title: Hopes for Civility in the Texas Senate Race
item.description: Readers respond to a David French column on James Talarico, a Christian Democrat running for the Senate in Texas. Also: Good health and common sense; the upside of small talk.

item.title: I Asked a Former Trump Official to Justify This War
item.description: Nadia Schadlow, a deputy national security adviser during Trump’s first term, explains how the U.S.-Israel war with Iran fits in with an “America first” agenda.
